activities.markdown:
# The 7+1 Structure

The classic approach to combining beats and fills:

- Play the groove for **7 beats** (almost 2 measures)
- **Fill** on beat 8 (the last beat of measure 2)
- Back to groove

This creates a natural 2-bar phrase — the most common unit in pop and rock music.

**Count:** 1 2 3 4 | 1 2 3 [FILL] | 1 2 3 4...

The fill anticipates the return of the groove. A great fill makes the listener want to come back to beat 1.

activities.title: Groove Bar — The Driving Beat
activities.description: The groove bar you repeat for 7 beats before the fill. Solid, consistent, and in the pocket.

activities.title: Fill Bar — Beat 4 Snare Burst
activities.description: The fill bar: groove through beats 1–3, then four 16th-note snare hits on beat 4 launch you back to the top.

activities.question: In a 2-bar groove-and-fill pattern, where does the fill typically occur?
activities.explanation: The standard 7+1 pattern plays the groove for 7 beats (bars 1 + first 3 beats of bar 2), then fills on beat 4 of bar 2 before returning to bar 1.
